title: Laboratory response to an infant with suspected measles vaccine associated fever and rash in Sri Lanka.
summary: A laboratory-based case investigation of an infant in Sri Lanka who presented with fever and rash following a measles-containing vaccine, aimed at differentiating vaccine-associated illness from wild-type measles.
key_findings: Measles vaccine type A (Edmonston-strain) detected via sequencing in throat swab.
Influenza A RNA detected in throat swab at day three.
ParvoB19 IgM detected in blood sample at six weeks post-onset.
population: One immunocompetent child (infant) in Sri Lanka
data_availability_statement: Data sharing not applicable to this article as no datasets were generated or analyzed during the current study.
